title: Integrated immunophenotypic approach to acute leukaemia classification
learning_objectives: Integrate morphology, flow cytometry, cytogenetics and molecular data
Select the most appropriate integrated diagnosis
Recognise the limits of immunophenotyping alone
clinical_vignette: A 45-year-old man presents with fatigue, bruising and recurrent infections. Bone marrow aspirate demonstrates 72% blasts. Rapid ancillary investigations are available before final karyotype.

morphology.peripheral_blood: Circulating myeloblasts with occasional Auer rods.
morphology.bone_marrow: Hypercellular marrow replaced by blasts with myeloid morphology.

flow_cytometry.interpretation: Definitive myeloid lineage with aberrant CD19 expression.

molecular_genetics: KIT mutation detected. FLT3-ITD negative. NPM1 negative.
integrated_laboratory_diagnosis: AML with RUNX1::RUNX1T1 fusion (core-binding factor AML).
question: What is the most appropriate integrated interpretation of these findings?
options: Mixed phenotype acute leukaemia because CD19 is expressed
AML with RUNX1::RUNX1T1; aberrant CD19 expression does not alter lineage assignment
B-lymphoblastic leukaemia with aberrant MPO expression
correct_answer: B. AML with RUNX1::RUNX1T1; aberrant CD19 expression does not alter lineage assignment
explanation: The presence of strong MPO together with RUNX1::RUNX1T1 establishes AML with a defining genetic abnormality. Aberrant CD19 expression is well recognised in RUNX1::RUNX1T1 AML and does not justify reclassification as mixed phenotype acute leukaemia.

why_other_options_are_wrong.A: CD19 alone is not lineage-defining for MPAL when definitive myeloid lineage is established.
why_other_options_are_wrong.C: B-ALL does not typically show strong MPO with RUNX1::RUNX1T1.
why_other_options_are_wrong.D: Acute undifferentiated leukaemia lacks lineage-defining markers.
why_other_options_are_wrong.E: AML with minimal differentiation is usually MPO negative.

frcpath_pearl: Always integrate morphology, immunophenotype and genetics. Genetics may define the AML subtype even when aberrant antigens are present.
take_home_message: Integrated diagnosis is the cornerstone of modern AML classification. Aberrant antigen expression must not override lineage-defining markers or AML-defining genetic abnormalities.
references: WHO Classification of Haematolymphoid Tumours, 5th edition
